name: Greece
contextBlurb: As of 2025, Greece applies the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) Regulation. Stablecoins are regulated as Electronic Money Tokens (EMTs) or Asset-Referenced Tokens (ARTs), with prior authorization, reserve, governance, disclosure and redemption-at-par requirements. The Hellenic Capital Market Commission (HCMC) is Greece’s national competent authority for most MiCA titles, while the Bank of Greece supervises e-money institutions relevant to EMT issuance. Greece opted for a 12-month MiCA transitional period (to Dec 30, 2025), after which only authorized CASPs/issuers may operate. There is no national legal-tender status for stablecoins; consumer safeguards flow from MiCA (segregated, high-quality reserves; transparency; enforceable redemptions).

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.

HCMC publishes Decision 8/1059 (Gov’t Gazette B’ 4479/20-08-2025) setting out the MiCA CASP authorisation application content & procedure. (Primary overview with Gazette ref from the Hellenic Bank Association; law-firm summary with decision number.)
HCMC Board adopts Decision 8/1059; HCMC posts an English summary (“Submitting an application for CASP”) aligning the forms with EU Level-2 acts.
Greece records its first crypto-asset seizure (freezing order by the Hellenic AML Authority) in the Bybit case.
EU publishes Implementing Reg. (EU) 2025/306 (standard forms/templates for CASP applications) used by NCAs like HCMC.
MiCA CASP regime becomes applicable EU-wide (stablecoin issuer rules for ART/EMT have applied since Jun 30, 2024).

news.headline: Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: As of 2025, Greece applies the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) Regulation. Stablecoins are regulated as Electronic Money Tokens (EMTs) or Asset-Referenced Tokens (ARTs), with prior authorization, reserve, governance, disclosure and redemption-at-par requirements. The Hellenic Capital Market Commission (HCMC) is Greece’s national competent authority for most MiCA titles, while the Bank of Greece supervises e-money institutions relevant to EMT issuance. Greece opted for a 12-month MiCA transitional period (to Dec 30, 2025), after which only authorized CASPs/issuers may operate. There is no national legal-tender status for stablecoins; consumer safeguards flow from MiCA (segregated, high-quality reserves; transparency; enforceable redemptions).
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: In Greece, stablecoins are classified under the EU’s MiCA as either Electronic Money Tokens (EMTs)—pegged 1:1 to a single official currency and issuable only by credit institutions or EMIs with e-money–style redemption rights—or Asset-Referenced Tokens (ARTs)—referencing a basket of assets with dist
